name: SoCo Aerial Collective
tagline: Passion. Power. Precision. Sonoma County aerial dance & fitness.
seoDescription: SoCo Aerial Collective is a Sonoma County aerial arts studio offering aerial silks and lyra classes for all levels at Vertex Climbing Center in Santa Rosa

images.heroAlt: A SoCo Aerial Collective student performing on aerial silks against an open sky

sections.heading: Find your strength in the air
sections.subheading: Aerial silks and lyra for every level, taught in small classes where students cheer each other on. Founded by Kristen Krup in 2017.

image.alt: A SoCo Aerial Collective student performing on aerial silks against an open sky

sections.heading: Built for aerialists of every level
body: Kristen Krup founded SoCo Aerial Collective in 2017 to share a craft she had already been studying for more than a decade. Her goal from day one was simple: build a space where anyone — first-timers and seasoned aerialists alike — could learn the art safely and feel genuinely encouraged.

That spirit is what students talk about most. Classes are kept small, capped at six, so every person gets real attention on the silks and lyra. The room fills with people of all levels cheering each other on, which is exactly the welcoming, supportive environment Kristen set out to create.
highlights: Founded in 2017 by aerialist Kristen Krup
Small group classes — capped at 6 students
Welcoming all levels, from first-timers to advanced
Classes at Vertex Climbing Center in Santa Rosa

members.name: Kristen Krup
members.bio: Founded SoCo Aerial Collective in 2017 after more than a decade studying aerial arts. Known by her students as a patient, encouraging teacher and a skilled performer.

items.title: Group Aerial Silks Classes
items.description: 60-minute classes at Vertex Climbing Center in Santa Rosa, with focused warm-ups, conditioning, and cool-downs. Capped at six students. Drop-in $35, four-week series $120.

items.title: Aerial Lyra (Hoop)
items.description: Work on the lyra alongside silks, building the fundamental body positions and control that aerial practice is built on.

items.title: Adult Classes — All Levels
items.description: From Intro/Beginner through Level II and beyond (ages 12+). Come as a complete beginner or keep progressing your skills with experienced coaching.

items.title: Kids Classes (Ages 8–12)
items.description: Level I/II and Intro/Level I classes built for young aerialists, with the same safe fundamentals and encouraging coaching.

items.title: Private Lessons
items.description: Solo or small-group private lessons outdoors in Forestville, Sunday through Thursday. $75 for 60 minutes (up to two people), or $60 for a 45-minute beginner session. Virtual and video consults available too.

items.title: Performances & Consultation
items.description: Aerial performances for events plus consultation services — get in touch to talk through what you have in mind.

items.title: Founded by a working aerialist
items.body: Kristen Krup started SoCo in 2017 after more than a decade on the silks, and still teaches the room herself.

items.title: Small classes, capped at six
items.body: Every student gets real eyes-on coaching and hands-on spotting — never lost in a crowd.

items.title: Truly all levels welcome
items.body: Walk in having never touched a silk, or keep progressing through Level II and beyond. Both belong here.

items.title: Silks and lyra, kids and adults
items.body: Programs for ages 8 and up on two apparatuses, taught at Vertex Climbing Center in Santa Rosa.

items.quote: SoCo Aerial Collective is without a doubt my favorite aerial studio. Kristen is an amazing aerial silk teacher and has truly fostered a wonderful environment to come and learn aerial skills.
items.author: SoCo Aerial student

items.quote: The students are positive girls and women who all cheer each other on. Kristen is an amazing instructor and performer — I recommend SoCo to any and all.
items.author: SoCo Aerial student

sections.heading: Come try your first class
sections.body: New to aerial or coming back to it, there’s a spot for you. Call (985) 285-3733 — we’re glad to help.

sections.heading: Ready to get off the ground?
sections.body: Book a class or ask about private lessons. Call (985) 285-3733 — we’re glad to help.
